mnemonics.title: IPC Types Memory Trick
mnemonics.content: Remember IPC mechanisms as 'PMSMSS' - PIPE, MESSAGE QUEUE, SHARED MEMORY, SIGNAL, SOCKET. Think 'PLEASE MAKE SURE MY SON SLEEPS' to remember the order.

mnemonics.title: Semaphore Operations
mnemonics.content: Remember: 'P'ull the value DOWN (wait/decrement), 'V'alidate value UP (signal/increment). Like 'P'ulling a rope DOWN to grab resources, 'V'isually seeing the count increase.

mnemonics.title: Critical Section Requirements
mnemonics.content: Remember as 'MPB': Mutual Exclusion (only ONE), Progress (keep moving), Bounded waiting (PATIENT). Think of a queue where each waits their TURN.

acronyms.usage: General mechanism for processes to exchange data and synchronize
Named pipe that maintains message order
Unique ID for processes, used in signal handling and process communication

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a water pipe connecting two tanks. Water (data) flows from one end to another. One process pours water in, another collects it. Only one direction flow allowed.
Think of a shared bulletin board in an office. Anyone can read and write to it directly. No messenger needed. But you need a system to prevent two people writing at the same time (synchronization).

Imagine a movie theater with a ticket counter. The semaphore value = available tickets. When someone takes a ticket, counter decreases. When they return, counter increases. Zero tickets = wait outside.

Signal is like a phone call - asynchronous notification. You don't know when it will come. When it rings, you stop current activity and answer (handler). Can ignore (ignore signal) or let it go to voicemail (default).

analogies.analogy: Message Queue is like postal service - you write a letter (message), send it, recipient receives it later. Shared Memory is like two people standing at the same whiteboard - instant access but need to coordinate not to write simultaneously.

Think of a restaurant kitchen. The chef (producer) puts food on a counter (buffer). The waiter (consumer) takes food from counter to serve customers. Counter has limited space - chef waits if full, waiter waits if empty.
Mutex is like a single key to a meeting room. Only one person can hold the key and enter. When done, they leave the key for next person. Without key, you cannot enter - ensures only one at a time.

Socket is like a phone socket on the wall. You need a phone (process) to connect. Once connected, you can talk (exchange data). Can be local extension (local socket) or external line (network socket).
